content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach avoids the major AoA anti-patterns across the conversation. They decline the client's repeated bait to give partner-picking advice or co-sign the 'rewire my attraction' framing (avoiding fixing-people). They don't validate the disdain for Marcus or collude with the victim frame. They don't deploy methodology jargon or diagnose her with attachment style even when she offers it on a platter (avoiding view-as-technique and dont-evangelize-the-work). Questions are spare and connective rather than rapid-fire investigative (avoiding wonder-as-strategy) — typically one focused question per turn, often reflecting her exact words back ('If I was just good enough at it'). The coach doesn't fight her defensiveness or try to dismantle the analytical deflections (avoiding dont-fight-identity / dont-fight-the-voice); they simply ask the next quiet question. No loving-to-transform vigor, no safety-as-control, no premature limiting-belief labeling. The turn 12 question about childhood is a slight risk for being 'therapist-y' (which the client immediately calls out), but it's offered as a genuine inquiry, not as a technique flourish, and the coach doesn't double down — they follow her into the felt sense in turn 14.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Notices the laugh, asks about felt experience of an available partner — refuses the 'how do I pick better' frame she's setting up."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reflects the dismissive wave without interpretation or accusation — does not push 'you choose this' as fact."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Offers a reframe as question, not as diagnosis — avoids view-as-technique."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'That's a very good explanation' — gentle non-collusion with the intellectual defense, then redirects to feeling. Doesn't fight the identity, doesn't validate it either."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses to engage the 'what was I doing wrong' victim hook. Turns attention to her own wanting."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Childhood pivot is slightly on-the-nose and she calls it 'therapist-y,' but the coach doesn't defend or evangelize, just lets her respond."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Drops the analytical thread and goes to embodied memory — avoids getting trapped in her self-diagnosis loop."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["looking-for-whats-wrong", "defensiveness-as-identity-signal"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["if-i-were-inherently-good", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "No anti-pattern trips. Coach did not yet ask the body-level question about being adored openly, but that's a methodology/territory issue, not an anti-pattern one."
}
